candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above the financial section, but it is generic and not identified as relating to COGS/cost of sales. No COGS-specific D&A label is shown.
Separate operating amortization line above the financial section, but it is generic and not identified as relating to COGS/cost of sales.
Could be a D&A-related label, but the preview shows it empty and there is a more explicit populated amortization row above.

variables.notes: The selected COGS bucket already consists of COGS/input-cost rows. The visible D&A rows are generic operating lines and are not labeled as COGS/COR-specific. To avoid unsupported allocation and double counting, no COGS_DA row is added separately.

candidates.why: Explicit separate operating depreciation line above the financial section and outside the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS rows. It appears to sit alongside, not inside, Personnel costs and Other operating expenses.
Explicit separate operating amortization line above the financial section and outside the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS rows. It is a clean amortization row without impairment.
Potential summary/alternate amortization label, but it is empty in the excerpt; the populated explicit amortization row is preferable.

variables.notes: R:c. The selected XSGA bucket includes Sales, General and Administration, Personnel costs, Other operating expenses, Raw Materials, and External Services. The statement shows depreciation and amortization as separate same-level operating lines after those buckets, so they do not appear embedded in the selected XSGA rows. 'Depreciation and impairment' was not chosen because impairment is out of scope and would risk double counting prohibited items. Manual review is needed because the D&A lines are generic and the excerpt does not clearly show whether any portion belongs to COGS rather than SG&A/overhead.
